pages.raw_text:
G. L. Smith, secretary of the AS
ISOLATION of Inland Empire Com
mercial Clubs, has received word
from the farmer who was selected
by the government to have charge
of the model farm" to be located
near here, that he had received the
plans of the government expert for
the operation of the farm and could
not accept them. He gave as a rea-
son the fact that he could not Sc
cure intelligent labor.


The model farm" was agitated
some time ago and after strenuous
efforts on the part of the chamber
Of commerce the agricultural
officials decided to establish one
not far from this city. Their plan
was to secure a farm established
for some time and get the consent
of the owner to run it along the
lines laid out by the government.
The farm was selected. the farmer
agreed to operate it as instructed
by government expert If the
plans appeared feasible to him, and
an expert was put to work on it.
A few days ago he submitted his
plans to the farmer, whose name
has been withheld.


The tiller of the soil, in rejecting
the governments proposition, said
/ that the plans were excellent in
every detail under ordinary condi
tons, but that the labor conditions
now existing throughout the state
